prompts.title: Daily Progress Report Template
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior site engineer with 10 years on Indian residential + commercial projects (L&T / Shapoorji Pallonji scale). Build a daily progress report (DPR) template for a 28-storey residential project in Mumbai. Cover: (1) date + weather + manpower (skilled + unskilled), (2) work executed today (per drawing reference), (3) materials received + consumed, (4) machinery deployed, (5) safety incidents, (6) quality checks done, (7) tomorrow's plan, (8) RFI / hold-points, (9) photos + signatures. Constraints: align with NBC + IS 4082; bilingual; mobile entry app friendly.

prompts.title: Material Estimation for RCC Slab
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior QS / site engineer. Estimate materials for a 220 sqm RCC slab (M30 grade, 150mm thick, Fe500 reinforcement). Cover: (1) concrete volume + 5% wastage, (2) cement bags (per m³ design mix), (3) sand + 20mm aggregate + 10mm aggregate (in cum + tonnes), (4) steel reinforcement (kg/m² estimate + bar bending schedule cross-check), (5) shuttering (sqm), (6) labour man-days, (7) INR cost per item from local Mumbai mandi rates, (8) total INR. Constraints: align with IS 456 + IS 10262 mix design; respect M30 yield; document for billing.

prompts.title: Safety Inspection Checklist for Site
prompts.prompt: Act as a site safety officer. Build a daily safety inspection checklist for a high-rise residential project. Cover: (1) PPE (helmet, harness, shoes), (2) scaffolding + edge protection, (3) excavation safety, (4) hot work permit, (5) electrical (earthing, ELCB), (6) fire (extinguishers, hydrant, evacuation), (7) crane + lifting, (8) housekeeping, (9) chemical storage, (10) workers' welfare (drinking water, toilets, first-aid). Constraints: align with BOCW Act 1996 + Factories Act + IS 14489; bilingual signage; documented incident-zero culture.

prompts.title: Vendor Communication for Material Delay
prompts.prompt: Act as a project engineer. Draft a 220-word firm-but-respectful email to my structural-steel vendor (Bhushan / Tata) about delayed delivery (7 days behind on a critical-path TMT bar consignment of 80 MT). Cover: (1) PO + invoice reference, (2) impact on slab pour schedule, (3) liquidated damages clause, (4) revised commitment ask (3-day window), (5) escalation to vendor leadership, (6) alternative-source warning, (7) sign-off + project manager copied. Constraints: align with contract terms; INR LD calculation; preserve relationship while protecting timeline.

prompts.title: Project Schedule (Microsoft Project / Primavera)
prompts.prompt: Act as a planning engineer. Build an 18-month construction schedule (in Primavera / MS Project format) for a 30-villa luxury residential project in Goa. Cover: (1) WBS (excavation, foundation, superstructure, MEP, finishes, landscaping, handover), (2) per-task duration + dependencies (FS, SS, FF), (3) critical path identification, (4) resource assignment, (5) milestones (foundation OK, slab OK, structure OK, possession), (6) risk buffer, (7) S-curve. Constraints: align with NBC + IS; respect monsoon (June-Sept); skilled-labour shortage near festivals; cite IRC for road work if applicable.

prompts.title: Concrete Cube Test Result Interpretation
prompts.prompt: Act as a quality engineer. Interpret 7-day + 28-day cube test results. Cubes from M30 mix poured for podium slab. 7-day strength avg: 22 N/mm² (target 22.5), 28-day avg: 36 N/mm² (target 33.5). Provide: (1) acceptance criteria per IS 456, (2) standard deviation analysis, (3) individual cube vs mean, (4) action if below acceptance, (5) report to client / consultant, (6) NDT correlation (rebound hammer), (7) corrective action if needed (core test, replacement). Constraints: align with IS 456 + IS 516; document in QA / QC log; signed by lab + engineer-in-charge.

prompts.title: Bar Bending Schedule (BBS)
prompts.prompt: Act as a structural engineer + QS hybrid. Prepare BBS for an RCC beam (B1, 9m × 0.45m × 0.6m). Provide: (1) main reinforcement (bottom + top — diameter, length, hook + bend), (2) stirrups (8mm @ 150 c/c, 200 c/c), (3) cut-length per bar with bend allowance, (4) total weight per bar size, (5) total steel kg + INR cost, (6) wastage 3%, (7) drawing cross-check. Constraints: align with IS 2502 + IS 1786 for Fe500; column-beam joint detailing per IS 13920; document for vendor cutting.

prompts.title: RFI Drafting for Design Clarification
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior site engineer. Draft an RFI to the structural designer for a discrepancy: GFC drawing shows a 200x600 column at gridline 7-A, but architectural plan shows a different shape. Cover: (1) RFI number + date + sender, (2) drawing reference, (3) discrepancy described factually, (4) implication on construction (already cast columns), (5) options (continue / re-design / approval), (6) urgency (3-day response — slab pour pending), (7) cost + schedule impact estimate. Constraints: align with FIDIC + Indian construction RFI norms; documented for variation order; cc'd to project manager.

prompts.title: Quantity Variation Statement (Billing)
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior QS. Prepare quantity variation statement for monthly billing on a ₹85 Cr project. Cover: (1) item-wise BoQ vs executed quantity, (2) variations (excess / less), (3) reasons (drawing change, site condition, design alt), (4) variation %, (5) extra item additions, (6) deduction items, (7) variation approval status (consultant + client signatures), (8) financial impact INR. Constraints: align with CPWD DSR / SoR + IRC if road; cap variations at 25% per contract; document for arbitration if any.

prompts.title: Pour Card / Concrete Pour Sequence
prompts.prompt: Act as a site engineer. Build a pour card for a 240 cum slab pour (podium level). Cover: (1) pre-pour checklist (reinforcement, formwork, cover, electrical conduits, plumber sleeves, MEP), (2) consultant approval signature, (3) RMC plant + supplier + truck schedule (15 trucks × 16 cum), (4) pour sequence (zone-by-zone), (5) sample collection (cubes, slump), (6) curing plan (28-day water + jute), (7) post-pour cleanup, (8) pour-card archival. Constraints: align with IS 456; respect monsoon contingency; site-mixed RMC compliance.

prompts.title: Subcontractor Performance Evaluation
prompts.prompt: Act as a project manager. Evaluate 3 subcontractors (RCC, MEP, finishes) on a ₹120 Cr residential project. Criteria (with weight): quality 25%, on-time 25%, safety 15%, manpower mobilisation 10%, billing accuracy 10%, rework 10%, communication 5%. Score each 1-5 with evidence; recommend renew / retain-with-improvement / replace. Constraints: align with industry norms; transparent feedback to subcontractor; documented; INR penalty / bonus based on score.

prompts.title: Snag List + Defect Liability
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior site engineer at handover stage. Prepare a 100-point snag list across 4 categories: (1) civil (cracks, plastering, finishing), (2) plumbing + sanitary, (3) electrical (light fixtures, switches, MCB), (4) finishes (tiles, paint, doors, windows). For each: location, severity, action, responsible subcontractor, target closure date. Plus DLP (Defect Liability Period) framework — 12 months, 5% retention. Constraints: align with NBC + IS standards; signed by client + consultant; INR retention against bank guarantee.
